title: From blue‑skies science to blue-chip technologies
gist: Sponsored Nature feature on TRACEGET, a new Vilnius (Lithuania) translational gene-technology center co-founded by CRISPR-Cas9 pioneer Virginijus Siksnys, funded as part of an 88.34M-euro national program; profiles Lithuania's fast-growing life-sciences sector and two spinout companies (Caszyme, Atrandi Biosciences).

insights.claim: Lithuania's life sciences sector is among the fastest-growing in Europe, with a stated national ambition to reach 5% of GDP by 2030.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Frames Lithuania/Vilnius as an emerging European biotech hub; watch for EU-funded translational infrastructure and spinouts as a sourcing ground for early-stage gene-tech deals.
insights.quote: Lithuania's life sciences sector is among the fastest-growing in Europe, with a national ambition to reach 5% of GDP by 2030.
insights.locator: It joins Caszyme... as part of the city's blooming biotech ecosystem

insights.claim: TRACEGET (Translational Center for Gene Technologies) consolidates eight core facilities including microfluidics, gene editing, single-cell sequencing and mass spectrometry in one Vilnius building, backed by roughly 14M euros of equipment.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Centralized shared-equipment translational hubs lower the capex barrier for gene-tech startups; a leading indicator of deal flow from the region.
insights.quote: In one building, TRACEGET will have eight core facilities, including microfluidics, gene editing, single-cell sequencing and mass spectrometry, supported by some €14 million of equipment.
insights.locator: Seeing the impact of basic research...

insights.claim: TRACEGET is the focus of Lithuania's 'Innovations for Health' mission within an 88.34M-euro national programme funded through the EU's Recovery and Resilience Plan and Lithuania's state budget.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Sizes the public funding behind the hub; EU RRF money is flowing into Eastern European biotech infrastructure.
insights.quote: part of a €88.34 million national programme, funded through the EU's Recovery and Resilience Plan and Lithuania’s state budget.
insights.locator: The centre is the focus of Lithuania's Innovations for Health mission

insights.claim: CRISPR-Cas9 pioneer Virginijus Siksnys, a biochemist at Vilnius University's Life Sciences Center, is spearheading TRACEGET to convert curiosity-driven academic research into commercial gene technologies.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: A founding-figure of CRISPR is anchoring the Vilnius translational push, lending credibility to the cluster as a deal-sourcing region.
insights.quote: Virginijus Šikšnys is one of the CRISPR-Cas9 pioneers and is spearheading the creation of TRACEGET to inspire future breakthroughs.
insights.locator: Seeing the impact of basic research...

insights.claim: Siksnys's semi-permeable-capsule and CRISPR technologies have spun out into commercial companies: Caszyme (co-founded 2017 to commercialize CRISPR-based technologies) and Atrandi Biosciences (formerly Droplet Genomics), which licensed Linas Mazutis's semi-permeable capsule platform.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Names the privately held Vilnius spinouts (Caszyme, Atrandi Biosciences) worth tracking for licensing, partnering, or financing activity.
insights.quote: The capsules have now been licensed to Atrandi Biosciences, a Vilnius-based company, co-founded by Mažutis originally as Droplet Genomics.

insights.claim: Mazutis's semi-permeable capsules (microscopic droplets with a thin reagent-permeable envelope) enable multi-step experimental workflows that droplet microfluidics cannot, and in 2025 his team used them to discover how leukaemia cells disrupt the immune system.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: A differentiated single-cell/microfluidics technology with a drug-discovery application; relevant for tracking Atrandi's competitive position vs droplet-microfluidics incumbents.
insights.quote: Using capsules, you can conduct multi-step workflows that you simply cannot do using droplet microfluidics
insights.locator: Working within TRACEGET, Mažutis's team is developing...
